post.content:
Give you an extra long list. Each element of the list is an array. For example: [[1, 5], [10, 16], [2, 7],..... [1000, 1027]]

The first number of arrays represents the driver's starting time and the second number is the arrival time for passengers. Question: Given the starting and ending time for online drivers from this input array, find the time interval where there is maximum drivers online. For example, the answer could be [16, 801]

You did not really clarify the question. Is the goal to find the maximum interval? Is it to find the most drivers in a given interval?

First problem solved easily by O(n) time O(1) space scan of values
Second problem solved easily by O(n) time O(n) space use of stack (meeting-rooms-ii problem)

Sort intervals by start times.  Go thought the sorted interval list and store the driver's data in a heap as (end,start).  
This way we always know the earliest end time among the drivers in the queue.
Also, store the start times of the drivers in the queue in  a SortedList (BST like structure with logN search,insert,delete).
This allows us to know the latest start times among the drivers in the heap.  Between the latest start and the earliest end is
the interval with the maximum drivers in the heap.  We just have to check if this max of drivers in the heap is better than the
global max.  If it is, we record the new global max.

Every time a new driver arrives, we check if his start time is greater than the min end time in the heap. if it is, the new driver 
takes the place of the min-end time driver on the heap.  We also expunge the min-end driver's start time from the SortedList.  
Before the driver replacement happens, we check the max-driver interval in the heap  against the global max.   
*Note that right after such a replacement takes place, the heap won't contain number of drivers more than the 
global max (which had just been recorded).*  This seems obvious, but I needed to persuade myself :-).

Don't forget to check heap max against the global max after the last driver's start is processed. 
It's possible that the max drivers are still out.  
(An alternative to doing this check would be to introduce a "dummy" driver with infinite start time) 
```
from typing import List
import heapq
from sortedcontainers import SortedList


def max_driver_interv(interv: List[List]):
    interv.sort()
    max_driv = in1 = in2 = 0
    pq = []
    sorted_starts_in_pq = SortedList()
    for st,en in interv:
        if pq:
            end_pq,start_pq = pq[0]
            if st >= end_pq:
                # a driver takes a passenger. Record max drivers, before the que size goes down.
                if len(pq) > max_driv:
                    max_driv = len(pq)
                    in1,in2 = sorted_starts_in_pq[-1], end_pq
                heapq.heappop(pq)
                # sorted_starts_in_pq.discard(start_pq)   # not needed, as later starts will cover the earlier ones
        heapq.heappush(pq,(en,st))
        sorted_starts_in_pq.add(st)
    if len(pq) > max_driv:
        in1, in2 = sorted_starts_in_pq[-1], pq[0][0]
    return in1,in2


if __name__ == '__main__':
    print(max_driver_interv([[1,10],[2,7],[3,19],[8,12],[10,20],[11,30],[19,35],[11,30],[14,40],[12,40]]))   # (14, 19)
    print(max_driver_interv([[1,2],[3,4]]))   # (1, 2)
    print(max_driver_interv([[1,14],[13,15]]))   # (13, 14)
    print(max_driver_interv([[1,10],[2,7],[3,19],[8,12],[10,20],[11,30]]))   # (11, 12)
    print(max_driver_interv([[23,40],[1,30],[7,25],[5,15],[10,18],[18,28],[6,17],[23,28]]))   # (10, 15)
```
